verses: sakhā⋅gaṇa saṅga, chāḍi jadunandana calatahi nāgara rāje bhāvinī manoratha, calala vipina pathe sādhite manoratha kāje catura śiromaṇi kāna heri jamunā jala, manamatha uthalala purala mūralī niśāna sirajilā tarīkhāni, prvāla mukutā āni mājhe mājhe hīrāra gāthani śikhipuccha guñjāchaḍā, rajata kāñcane moḍā keroyāle rajata kiṅkiṇī tapana⋅tanayā⋅tīre, taruṇi looyā phire vidagara rāja govinda dāsa bhaṇe, ki ānanda haila mane runu jhunu nūpūra bāja
en_translation:
Leaving the company of His friends, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the King of Lovers, walks along. Contemplating His desires, He walks on the forest path to fulfill them.

Lord Kṛṣṇa, the crest-jewel of the clever, seeing the waters of the Yamunā, feels the desires of love overflow, and the sound of His flute fills the air.

He fashioned a boat, bringing coral and pearls, with diamonds strung between them. It was adorned with peacock feathers, guñjā berries, silver, and gold, and the oars were decorated with silver bells.

On the banks of the Yamunā, the clever King takes the young gopīs around. Govinda Dāsa says: "What bliss arose in His mind as the anklets tinkled runu-jhunu."
